winning_idea: RefiRail — one-click atomic lending-position refinance on Sui (Navi -> Suilend in ONE PTB via DeepBook flash loan)

quality_scores.architecture: 10/10 metrics PASS; cross-doc PRD<->ARCH 4/4 PASS; THESIS-3/4 PASS; 30 files, 1680 lines
quality_scores.implementation_plan: 7/7 metrics PASS; 18 decision trees; 6 phase gates; 853 lines
quality_scores.cross_doc_audit: 9/9 cross-doc checks PASS; THESIS-1..5 all PASS

thesis.winning_argument: RefiRail is the only idea that satisfies the entire criteria journey at once — real recurring use, not a clone, plugs into existing on-chain liquidity (no market to bootstrap), deeply ecosystem-relevant — and its feasibility is source-verified as buildable in pure TypeScript with zero net-new Move.
thesis.demo_obligation: In 3 minutes the judge must WITNESS a real lending position move from one protocol to a cheaper one in ONE atomic transaction — a single Suiscan digest showing flash-borrow -> repay source -> withdraw collateral -> deposit destination -> borrow -> flash-repay — with before/after rate + health factor, and the collateral provably intact (recovered, not spent).
thesis.hero_flow: connect wallet -> see your Navi position at the higher rate -> one click 'Refinance to Suilend' -> one atomic PTB -> position now at the lower rate.
invariants: The refinance is ONE atomic PTB — never a multi-tx sequence that leaves the user exposed.
Reverts on unhealthy end-state — never leaves a worse/liquidatable position.
Real protocols + real liquidity (mainnet, small amounts) — no mocked lending; the demo proof is a real Suiscan digest.
Minimal/zero net-new Move — TypeScript PTB composition IS the build.
drift_tripwires: if it becomes a generic lending dashboard / rate-aggregator instead of the one-click atomic MOVE, that is drift.
if a custom Move wrapper is built before the pure-TS PTB works end-to-end, that is drift.
if the demo is only a simulation with no real on-chain transaction, that is drift (the Suiscan link is the credibility anchor).

unknowns: Suilend reserve indices (resolve at runtime via findReserveArrayIndex)
Navi SUI/USDC poolIds (re-confirm Day 1 via getPools)
verified_patterns: Suilend 3.0.x init takes SuiGrpcClient not SuiClient (BREAKING)
DeepBook DEEPBOOK_PACKAGE_ID 0x0e735f8c... + SUI_USDC pool 0xe05dafb5... USDC=quote; borrowQuoteAsset/returnQuoteAsset; fee=0; hot-potato; human units; curried tx.add
Navi SUI assetId=0, native USDC assetId=10 (1=bridged, avoid); repayCoinPTB(tx,id,coin,{amount}), withdrawCoinPTB(tx,id,amount)->Coin; global-storage no accountCap
Native USDC 0xdba34672...::usdc::USDC (6dp) VERIFIED x4; wormhole 0x5d4b3025...::coin::COIN AVOID
Pyth SUI/USD 0x23d73151..., USDC/USD 0xeaa020c6...; Pyth State 0x1f931023..., Wormhole State 0xaeab97f9... (Suilend refreshAll handles Pyth internally)
unverified_patterns: DeepBook constants pinned to HEAD — verify installed constants.js before paste
No standalone open-source repo pins the exact Suilend one-PTB sequence in one file (source+Move+looping-precedent is the evidence)

project_type_rationale: Next.js + dapp-kit frontend + TS PTB-composition library + headless Node scripts; on-chain integration with 3 live protocols; ZERO net-new Move (hasContracts=false). User flows: wallet connect, view position, one-click refinance.
